title: House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 (UK) – Apply Now
summary: Are you a Year 12 student in the UK looking to gain real-world experience inside Parliament? Applications are now open for the House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 — a unique opportunity to explore careers in public service, policy, and governance. Delivered by the House of Lords , this one-week immersive programme gives students a behind-the-scenes look at how Parliament operates and how laws are shaped in the UK. Key Details at a Glance Host: House of Lords Programme Type: Work Experience (Social Mobility Programme) Location: London, United Kingdom Duration: 1 Week Eligibility: Year 12 students (UK) Application Deadline: 22 April 2026 Programme Overview The House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme offers selected students the chance to: Experience Parliament beyond what you see in the news Learn how the House of Lords functions in practice Engage directly with professionals across departments Participate in real workplace activities and projects This is an ideal opportunity for students interested in politics, governance, communications, or public service careers . The House of Lords is running a social mobility work experience programme for Year 12 students. The House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme will offer students the opportunity to go beyond the headlines and experience Parliament from the inside. Over one exciting week, participants will explore how the House of Lords operates. The programme includes: Working alongside professionals in key departments Taking part in hands-on tasks and real workplace activities Shadowing staff and seeing how different roles come together behind the scenes Joining interactive workshops designed to build your skills and confidence. What you ll gain: A clear understanding of careers in Parliament Practical workplace experience you can talk about in applications Confidence, connections and inspiration. Whether you’re curious about politics, interested in public service or just want to try something new, this programme could be for you. Offices involved Property and Office Services This team is responsible for front of house services, houskeeping, heritage cleaning and accommodation as well as managing contracts for the parliamentary estate and supporting work on ceremonial events and maintenance projects. Black Rod s department The team plays a leading role in all ceremonial events such as the State Opening of Parliament and state visits, as well as supporting the day to day running of the House of Lords and controlling access to the chamber. Clerk of the Parliament s Office The office supports the Clerk of the Parliaments to carry out their duties as head of the Administration, plus other key officeholders such as the Senior Deputy Speaker, Convenor of the Crossbench Peers, Clerk Assistant and Chief Operating Officer. The office also includes teams responsible for governance, internal audit, interparliamentary relations, and Restoration and Renewal of the Palace of Westminster. Committees The Commitee Office provides support for Lords select committees; small groups of members investigating areas of public policy. The team provides committees with the policy, procedural, logistical, administrative and legal support they need to carry out their work. Communications Communications is responsible for explaining the role and work of the House of Lords to the public, increasing awareness and improving understanding of the the value it adds. The team includes press and media, digital communications, engagement, public enquiries and internal communications. How to apply For full details and to submit your application, visit the Futures for All website https://finder.futuresforall.org/opportunity-detail/6F87378B-EBBA-4F4A-04AD-08DE8A5A1670 The deadline to apply is Wednesday 22 April 2026. About the House of Lords The House of Lords is the upper chamber of the UK Parliament. It is independent from, and complements the work of, the elected House of Commons. The Lords shares the task of making and shaping laws and checking and challenging the work of the government. Application Link: https://finder.futuresforall.org/opportunity-detail/6F87378B-EBBA-4F4A-04AD-08DE8A5A1670 For more opportunities for young people in the UK and Europe please visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Europe The post House of Lords Social Mobility Work Experience Programme 2026 (UK) – Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026– Open for Applications
summary: The 2026 Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships are now open, offering €399.05 million in funding to support researchers worldwide. These prestigious fellowships provide advanced training, international mobility, and career development opportunities for PhD holders across all disciplines. Key Details at a Glance Programme: MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 Funding: €399.05 million Deadline: 9 September 2026 Launch Date: 9 April 2026 Projects Funded: ~1,600 expected Duration: European Fellowships: Up to 24 months Global Fellowships: 12–24 months + 12-month return phase in Europe Fields: All disciplines (including Euratom research) Eligibility: PhD holders with up to 8 years of research experience About the MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships The MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships are among Europe’s most competitive research funding programmes. They support researchers in gaining new skills , expanding networks, and advancing careers through international, interdisciplinary, and inter-sectoral mobility . Fellows work with leading institutions worldwide and benefit from strong research collaboration and mentorship. Types of Fellowships 1. European Postdoctoral Fellowships Open to researchers of any nationality Hosted in EU Member States or Horizon Europe associated countries Duration: Up to 24 months 2. Global Postdoctoral Fellowships Open to EU nationals or long-term residents Research conducted in a third country (12–24 months) Includes a mandatory 12-month return phase in Europe Additional Opportunity: Non-Academic Placement Fellows can request an additional 6-month placement in a non-academic organisation (e.g., industry, NGOs, public sector) in Europe, helping bridge research and real-world application. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold a PhD at the time of application Have a maximum of 8 years of research experience post-PhD Apply jointly with a host organisation and supervisor Eligible host organisations include: Universities Research institutes Companies and SMEs Government or public institutions NGOs, hospitals, museums, and other organisations For Global Fellowships , applicants must also secure a host organisation in a third country . Who Should Apply? The programme is open to: Researchers from any country Those seeking to restart their careers Researchers displaced by conflict Researchers aiming to reintegrate into Europe Conditions for researchers and organisations MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships are open to postdoctoral researchers from all over the world, of any nationality and at any career stage, with a maximum of 8 years of research experience after their PhD. Some exceptions and specific conditions apply, for instance for Global Postdoctoral Fellowships and Euratom research. Researchers must develop an application with their prospective supervisor and apply together with their future host organisation, which can be a university a research institution or facility a company, small or medium-sized enterprise a government, public institution, or body a museum, hospital, or NGO any other organisation based in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe associated country . Researchers applying to Global Fellowships will need to seek the commitment of an organisation based in a third country , as they will carry out their research there for a period of between 12 and 24 months. The call is open to researchers wishing to reintegrate in Europe, to those displaced by conflict, as well as to researchers with high potential who are seeking to restart their careers. ERA Fellowships (Additional Funding Opportunity) Applicants to European Fellowships hosted in “widening countries” can opt into ERA Fellowships . Around 40 additional fellowships available Targeted at excellent applicants not funded due to budget limits Find the eligible host countries for ERA Fellowships . Indicative Timeline 9 April 2026: Call opens 9 September 2026: Application deadline February 2027: Results announced (TBC) Feb–May 2027: Grant agreements signed May 2027: Projects begin Why Apply for MSCA Fellowships? Access world-class research environments Gain international experience Strengthen your academic and non-academic career prospects Build global networks and collaborations Enhance your research impact and visibility 30 Years of MSCA Excellence 2026 marks the 30th anniversary of the MSCA programme . Since 1996, MSCA has: Supported 150,000+ researchers Contributed to 23 Nobel Prize winners Strengthened global research collaboration and mobility How to Apply Applicants must develop a proposal jointly with their host institution and supervisor. Start early and ensure your proposal demonstrates: Strong research excellence Clear impact Quality training and supervision Find out more and apply The MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 represent one of the best-funded and most prestigious opportunities for early-career researchers globally. With nearly 1,600 projects expected to be funded , this is a major opportunity to advance your research career on an international stage. Postdoctoral Fellowships call: 6 steps to prepare your application Visit the 2026 Postdoctoral Fellowship call page Find out more on how to apply to MSCA The post Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026– Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD on Youth & AI at KU Leuven (IAmAI Project) – Apply by May 31, 2026
summary: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming education, identity development, and how young people interact with the world. Yet, many AI systems are still designed without actively involving the very individuals they impact the most—youth. A groundbreaking fully funded PhD opportunity at KU Leuven aims to change that narrative by placing young people at the center of AI development through the innovative IAmAI Project: Youth in the Loop of AI Developments . About the PhD Opportunity This four-year doctoral position is hosted at the Centre for Methodology of Educational Sciences (CMPO), within the Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences. The research is led by Melissa De Smet , an expert in qualitative and mixed-methods research. The project focuses on developing participatory methodologies that bridge the gap between how AI systems are built and how young people actually learn, grow, and experience the world. Why This PhD Stands Out This is not a conventional PhD. It is designed for researchers who want to: Explore how AI can be co-created with young people Develop innovative qualitative and participatory research methods Influence ethical AI design and policy recommendations Work within a multidisciplinary, international research environment By focusing on co-creation, this project ensures that AI systems better reflect the real, dynamic experiences of youth rather than relying solely on static datasets. Research Focus The IAmAI project tackles a critical issue in AI development—the mismatch between generalized data models and individual lived experiences. Your research will: Investigate interactions between youth and AI systems Develop and test participatory research methodologies Reimagine personalization in AI tools such as learning platforms and generative AI chatbots Translate findings into actionable policy and methodological recommendations Key Responsibilities During the PhD, you will: Conduct high-quality qualitative research Publish in internationally recognized academic journals Present findings at global conferences Support teaching in psychology and educational sciences programs What You Will Gain This position offers a highly supportive academic environment with opportunities to grow professionally and personally: Full funding for four years Access to international conferences, workshops, and training Collaboration with leading interdisciplinary researchers Additional benefits such as transport reimbursement, insurance, and eco-vouchers A dynamic, multicultural research setting in Leuven, Belgium Ideal Candidate Profile Applicants should have: A Master’s degree in educational sciences, psychology, or a related field Strong interest in qualitative research methods Passion for youth engagement and participatory approaches Interest in AI and ethical research practices Excellent communication skills Fluency in Dutch and English Experience in academic writing and familiarity with qualitative data collection and analysis are strong advantages. Location and Start Date Location: Leuven, Belgium Start Date: October 2026 (flexible upon agreement) Application Process Applicants are required to submit: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Motivation letter Academic transcripts Optional writing sample (thesis or research paper) Applications must be submitted via the official KU Leuven online platform. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for this PhD position is May 31, 2026 (23:59 CET) . Final Thoughts This PhD offers a rare opportunity to contribute to the ethical and inclusive future of AI by directly involving young people in its development. If you are passionate about research, education, and technology—and want to make a meaningful impact—this program provides the perfect platform. The post Fully Funded PhD on Youth AI at KU Leuven (IAmAI Project) – Apply by May 31, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Zeitz MOCAA & UWC Museum Fellowship Programme 2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging African Art Professionals
summary: The Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa (Zeitz MOCAA), in partnership with the University of the Western Cape (UWC), has officially opened applications for its prestigious Museum Fellowship Programme 2027. This highly competitive, fully immersive fellowship is designed to empower the next generation of African art and museum professionals through hands-on training, academic development, and real-world experience. If you are passionate about contemporary African art, curatorial practice, and museum studies, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to shape the future of the continent’s cultural and heritage landscape. About the Fellowship Programme The Zeitz MOCAA UWC Museum Fellowship Programme is a 12-month, full-time programme running from February 2027 to January 2028 in Cape Town, South Africa. It combines practical museum experience with academic coursework, culminating in a BA Honours degree from UWC upon successful completion. Fellows will gain direct exposure to various museum departments, including: Curatorial Practice Exhibition Design Registrar Collection Management Art Education Institutional Advancement Participants will work closely with industry experts, contributing to exhibitions, research, public programming, and publishing initiatives. What the Fellowship Offers This fellowship is designed to provide both academic and professional growth. Selected fellows will receive: Full tuition coverage Accommodation Monthly stipend Basic health insurance Hands-on experience in a leading contemporary art museum Academic mentorship and supervision Please note that travel and visa costs are not covered. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: Hold a Bachelor’s degree in art, heritage, museum studies, or a related humanities field (or equivalent experience) Demonstrate knowledge of contemporary African art and heritage Have 2–3 years of relevant work experience Possess strong writing and public speaking skills Show clear interest in an art-related career Be proficient in English Be a citizen of an African country Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents: One-page motivation letter Full CV 250-word biography A minimum 1000-word original writing sample Letter of recommendation High-resolution portrait photo Academic certificates and transcripts All documents must be submitted in English and follow the required naming format. Important Application Guidelines Only the first 100 applications will be considered Applications must be submitted online Incomplete or late submissions will not be accepted Applicants must carefully follow all instructions and word limits Deadline The application deadline is 30 June 2026 . Successful applicants will be contacted by early August 2026. Why You Should Apply This fellowship is more than just a training programme—it is a gateway into Africa’s evolving art ecosystem. Fellows will gain invaluable experience, expand their professional networks, and develop the skills needed to lead in curatorial practice, research, and museum management. Whether you aim to work in galleries, museums, cultural institutions, or academia, this programme provides a strong foundation for a successful career in the arts. How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the online application form and upload all required documents before the deadline. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the limited number of slots considered. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026: Up to $500,000 in Funding for Global Internet Research
summary: The Internet Society Foundation has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated Research Grant Program 2026 , offering funding between $200,000 and $500,000 to support innovative research projects that explore the evolving role of the Internet in society. This prestigious global program is designed to empower researchers and institutions to generate practical, open-access knowledge that can shape policies, improve digital ecosystems, and create a more inclusive, secure, and sustainable Internet for everyone. 💡 About the Research Grant Program The Research Grant Program supports forward-thinking research aligned with the Internet Society Foundation’s 2030 strategic vision —a world where everyone has access to affordable, reliable, and secure Internet connectivity. Over the past five years, the program has: Funded 70+ research projects Invested over $22 million globally Influenced policy decisions, industry practices, and technological innovation The program places strong emphasis on collaboration, diversity, and real-world impact , particularly encouraging researchers from the Global South and underrepresented communities. 🎯 Key Focus Areas Applicants must align their proposals with at least one of the following themes: 1. Inclusive Internet Research addressing barriers such as affordability, gender inequality, geography, and digital literacy that limit Internet access and participation. 2. Greening the Internet Projects exploring the environmental impact of Internet infrastructure, energy consumption, and climate resilience. 3. Measuring Meaningful Connectivity Studies focused on redefining connectivity beyond access—examining how Internet use enables real participation in economic, social, and civic life. 4. A Trustworthy Internet Research on Internet security, reliability, accountability, and emerging risks that affect trust in digital systems. 💰 Funding Details Up to $200,000 for independent researchers Up to $500,000 for organizations and institutions Funding supports applied, open research that addresses real-world challenges and is accessible to the global scientific community. ✅ Eligibility Criteria Independent researchers must hold a postgraduate degree (Master’s or PhD) and demonstrate prior research output Public research institutions must be non-profit (or equivalent) and aligned with the Foundation’s mission Projects must comply with ethical standards and local regulations Strong encouragement for applicants from underrepresented and Global Majority communities 🧠 Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Alignment with thematic focus areas Innovation and originality Practical impact and policy relevance Collaboration and interdisciplinary approach Intersectional research design 📅 Important Deadline Application Opens: April 7, 2026 Application Deadline: May 22, 2026 (21:00 UTC) Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. 📝 How to Apply Applicants must submit their proposals through the Foundation’s online grant management system (Fluxx). Key steps include: Register on the platform at least 24 hours before the deadline Prepare and submit a complete application in English, French, or Spanish Ensure compliance with all eligibility and documentation requirements APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grantopportunities, click here . 📢 Why You Should Apply This program is more than just funding—it’s an opportunity to: Influence global Internet policy and governance Contribute to cutting-edge research and innovation Collaborate with a diverse, international research community Create real-world impact in digital inclusion and sustainability 🔗 Useful Links Apply via the official platform (Fluxx) Attend info sessions (April 20 21, 2026) Contact: foundation@isocfoundation.org 📌 Final Thoughts If you are passionate about shaping the future of the Internet and addressing global digital challenges, the Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026 offers a powerful platform to turn your ideas into impactful solutions. The post Internet Society Foundation Research Grant Program 2026: Up to $500,000 in Funding for Global Internet Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded German Bundestag IPS Africa Scholarship 2027 in Berlin (Stipend + Travel Covered)
summary: The German Bundestag is offering an incredible opportunity for politically and socially engaged young graduates from sub-Saharan Africa to participate in the prestigious International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS Africa) Programme 2027 in Berlin, Germany. This fully funded programme is designed to equip emerging leaders with firsthand knowledge of parliamentary democracy, governance, and political decision-making processes within one of Europe’s most influential legislative institutions. About the IPS Africa Programme Launched in 2020 under the patronage of the President of the German Bundestag, the IPS Africa Programme aims to empower young African leaders who are passionate about shaping the democratic future of their countries. Each year, up to 24 participants are selected from eligible African countries to take part in a four-week intensive programme in Berlin . Programme Duration Start Date: 4 January 2027 End Date: 2 February 2027 What to Expect Participants will gain practical and theoretical insights into Germany’s parliamentary system through: A one-week placement in the office of a Member of the German Bundestag Exposure to legislative processes and political decision-making Workshops on: Parliamentary democracy Anti-corruption strategies Intercultural communication Project management Discussions on: Pluralism and diversity Minority protection Historical memory and political responsibility The programme also supports fellows in developing projects that promote democracy and civic engagement in their home countries. Financial Benefits The IPS Africa Programme is fully funded and includes: Monthly stipend of €700 Free accommodation in Berlin Round-trip travel expenses Health, accident, and liability insurance coverage Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be citizens of one of the participating countries Be under 30 years old by January 1, 2027 Hold a university degree Have at least B2-level proficiency in German Demonstrate strong interest in political and social issues Show active engagement in civic or political activities Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Proof of German language proficiency (B2 or higher) Motivation letter (in German, max. 2 pages, signed) Copy of passport or national ID University degree certificate (English or German) Letter of recommendation (German or English) Important: All application materials must be submitted in German. Application Process Applications must be submitted through the official IPS digital platform. Email submissions are no longer accepted. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview at a German diplomatic mission. Final selection is based on: Professional qualifications Social and political engagement Language proficiency Intercultural competence Application Deadline Application Period: 1 April – 15 May 2026 Late applications will not be considered. Important Note Applicants from Namibia and South Africa may also apply for the five-month IPS Global Programme. While it is possible to apply for both, only one programme can be attended. Contact Information For inquiries, contact: ips-afrika@bundestag.de The post Fully Funded German Bundestag IPS Africa Scholarship 2027 in Berlin (Stipend + Travel Covered) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the CLA Foundation Scholarship 2026 and Receive $15,000 for Your Studies
summary: The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship 2026 offers students across the United States the chance to receive $15,000 in funding to support their undergraduate or graduate studies in business, finance, technology, and other emerging professional fields. Open to high school seniors, current college students, and graduate students, this scholarship aims to support future leaders shaping the evolving landscape of professional services. Administered by Scholarship America, the program identifies high-potential students who demonstrate academic achievement, leadership, and a clear commitment to their chosen career path. With applications open until May 19, 2026 , this opportunity provides financial support while helping students advance toward careers in high-demand sectors. About the Program The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship Program is designed to invest in students pursuing careers in both traditional and emerging professional service industries. These include fields such as accounting, finance, and business, as well as rapidly growing areas like artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, and regulatory technology. The scholarship is funded by the CLA Foundation and administered by Scholarship America, one of the largest scholarship management organizations in the United States. Through this collaboration, the program ensures a rigorous and fair selection process, identifying students who demonstrate both academic excellence and future leadership potential. Recipients are awarded a one-time scholarship of $15,000, which can be used to support tuition and other educational expenses at accredited two-year or four-year colleges, universities, or graduate schools. Why This Opportunity Matters The professional services sector is undergoing rapid transformation due to advancements in technology and shifting global demands. Fields such as artificial intelligence, data analytics, and digital transformation are reshaping how businesses operate, creating new opportunities for skilled professionals. This scholarship supports students entering these evolving fields by reducing financial barriers and enabling them to focus on their education and career development. By investing in both traditional and emerging disciplines, the program helps prepare a new generation of professionals equipped to lead innovation and drive economic growth. In addition, the scholarship recognizes the importance of leadership, community involvement, and resilience. By selecting students who demonstrate these qualities, the program contributes to building a workforce that is not only skilled but also socially responsible and community-oriented. What Participants Will Gain The CLA Foundation Opportunity Scholarship provides both financial and professional advantages. Recipients receive $15,000 in funding, which can significantly reduce the cost of higher education and ease financial stress. Beyond financial support, the scholarship enhances students’ academic and professional profiles. Being selected as a recipient signals strong leadership potential and academic achievement, which can open doors to future opportunities. Participants may also gain access to internship opportunities with CLA-affiliated offices, providing valuable hands-on experience in professional services. Additionally, the recognition associated with the scholarship can strengthen future applications for jobs, graduate programs, and other competitive opportunities. Who Can Apply The scholarship is open to students in the United States who meet the following criteria: Applicants must be high school seniors, graduates, or current undergraduate or graduate students planning to enroll full-time in an accredited institution for the upcoming academic year. Eligible fields of study include accounting, business, finance, and a wide range of emerging disciplines such as computer science, data analytics, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and related areas. Applicants must have a minimum GPA of 2.5 on a 4.0 scale or equivalent. Candidates should also demonstrate leadership, participation in school or community activities, work experience, and clear educational and career goals. Financial need is considered as part of the selection process. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must complete the online application through the official Scholarship America platform. The application requires submission of a complete academic transcript, a personal statement outlining educational and career goals, and one recommendation form. Applicants must ensure all materials are submitted electronically before the deadline. Incomplete applications will not be considered. The deadline to apply is May 19, 2026, at 3:00 PM CT . Applicants will be notified of the results in July, and scholarship funds will be distributed in early August. This scholarship provides a valuable opportunity for students to invest in their education, build their professional future, and contribute to the evolving world of professional services. To learn mire and apply, visit the Scholarship America website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the CLA Foundation Scholarship 2026 and Receive $15,000 for Your Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply to Join the AWARD Reviewer Pool and Shape Africa’s Future Leaders in Agrifood Systems
summary: The AWARD Reviewer Pool offers experienced professionals the opportunity to contribute to the selection of Africa’s next generation of leaders in agrifood systems while gaining insight into emerging innovations in climate resilience and gender equality. Applications are open until 30 April 2026 for experts interested in supporting high-impact fellowship programs across the continent. African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) is inviting qualified professionals to join its reviewer pool for flagship fellowship initiatives. This opportunity is designed for individuals with strong expertise in agriculture, policy, climate change, and leadership development who are committed to advancing inclusive and sustainable development across Africa. About the Program The AWARD Reviewer Pool plays a critical role in maintaining the quality and integrity of fellowship selection processes. Reviewers are responsible for evaluating applications submitted by candidates across AWARD’s programs, which aim to build leadership capacity and strengthen agrifood systems throughout Africa. Rather than being a full-time role, this opportunity is a flexible, short-term engagement that allows professionals to contribute alongside their existing responsibilities. Selected reviewers will assess a small number of applications using structured evaluation criteria and provide objective, constructive feedback to support fair decision-making. The reviewer role also includes participation in virtual briefing sessions and adherence to clear guidelines on confidentiality, ethics, and conflict of interest. Through this process, AWARD ensures that its fellowship cohorts are composed of high-potential individuals capable of driving meaningful change. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa faces complex challenges related to climate change, food security, and sustainable agriculture. Addressing these challenges requires strong leadership, innovation, and inclusive approaches that prioritize gender equality and community resilience. By joining the AWARD Reviewer Pool, professionals play a direct role in identifying and supporting individuals who are working to transform agrifood systems across the continent. This contribution helps ensure that fellowship programs select candidates with the potential to drive impactful solutions in research, policy, and practice. Additionally, the reviewer pool strengthens the credibility and transparency of selection processes. By involving diverse experts with regional and thematic knowledge, AWARD enhances the quality of its programs and promotes equity in access to opportunities. What Participants Will Gain Serving as a reviewer offers both professional and intellectual benefits. Participants gain exposure to innovative ideas, research proposals, and emerging leaders working in agrifood systems and climate adaptation. Reviewers also deepen their analytical and evaluation skills by engaging with structured assessment frameworks and providing detailed feedback. This experience can strengthen their expertise in selection processes, grant evaluation, and program design. In addition, reviewers become part of a broader community of professionals committed to advancing sustainable development in Africa. This engagement can lead to new collaborations, knowledge exchange, and long-term involvement in AWARD’s initiatives. A modest honorarium is provided in recognition of the time and expertise contributed by reviewers. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to professionals with strong experience in areas such as agricultural research, agrifood systems, climate change, policy, gender, and leadership development. Applicants should hold a Master’s degree, PhD, or equivalent experience, along with demonstrated expertise in research, academia, policy, or program leadership. Strong analytical and critical thinking skills are essential, as well as the ability to provide objective and high-quality assessments. Experience reviewing proposals, fellowships, or grants is considered an advantage, as is proficiency in English and/or French. Application Process and Deadline Interested candidates must submit their application through the official Reviewer Expression of Interest form available online. Applications submitted through other means will not be considered. The deadline to apply is 30 April 2026 . Selected reviewers will be contacted with further details regarding assignments, timelines, and onboarding processes. This is a valuable opportunity for experienced professionals to contribute their expertise to high-impact programs while supporting the development of future leaders working to address some of Africa’s most pressing challenges. To learn more and apply, visit the official African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Join DC Action’s Board of Directors: Lead Change for Youth in Washington, DC
summary: The DC Action Young Adult Board Member opportunity offers young leaders aged 18–30 the chance to gain governance experience while shaping policies that impact children and youth across Washington, DC. This unique leadership role is designed for individuals who are passionate about advocacy, equity, and community impact, and who want to contribute their voice to decision-making processes that influence real change. DC Action is currently seeking applications from motivated young adults ready to step into leadership roles and support its mission of ensuring that all children and youth in the District of Columbia grow up safe, resilient, and empowered. About the Program DC Action is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving outcomes for children and youth through research, advocacy, and collaboration. Formed through the merger of DC Action for Children and the DC Alliance of Youth Advocates, the organization works to unify efforts across sectors and ensure a more coordinated approach to supporting young people. As a Young Adult Board Member, selected candidates will serve on the organization’s Board of Directors and contribute to governance, oversight, and strategic decision-making. Board members play an important role in guiding the organization’s direction, ensuring accountability, and strengthening its advocacy efforts. This position is designed not only to support the organization but also to provide young leaders with hands-on experience in nonprofit governance and policy engagement. Why This Opportunity Matters Young people are often the most affected by policies related to education, housing, health, and economic opportunity, yet they are frequently underrepresented in decision-making spaces. This opportunity addresses that gap by placing young leaders directly at the table where key decisions are made. By joining the DC Action Board, young adults can bring their lived experiences and perspectives into discussions that shape programs and policies. This contributes to more inclusive, equitable, and effective solutions for communities. In addition, the organization’s commitment to racial equity and its focus on addressing systemic barriers ensures that board members are engaging in work that has long-term impact on social justice and community wellbeing. What Participants Will Gain Serving as a Young Adult Board Member offers both personal and professional development opportunities. Participants will gain valuable leadership experience by contributing to governance and strategic planning processes. They will also build skills in advocacy, event planning, and fundraising—key competencies for careers in nonprofit leadership, public policy, and community development. Board members will have the opportunity to expand their professional networks, collaborate with experienced leaders, and gain insight into how organizations operate at a strategic level. In addition, selected participants will receive a modest quarterly stipend, providing financial support while they contribute their time and expertise. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to young adults aged 18 to 30 who are passionate about improving outcomes for children and youth in Washington, DC. Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in advocacy, leadership, and community engagement. While prior board experience is not required, candidates should be committed to contributing actively to the organization’s mission and participating in governance activities. DC Action encourages applications from individuals with diverse backgrounds and perspectives, particularly those from communities that are directly impacted by systemic inequities. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must submit a resume and a cover letter outlining their interest, experience, and motivation for joining the board. Review the official requirements and responsibilities on the DC Action website . The deadline to apply is 15 May 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to submit thoughtful and well-prepared applications that clearly demonstrate their commitment to youth advocacy and leadership. This role provides a meaningful pathway for young leaders to influence policy, build skills, and contribute to a stronger future for children and youth in Washington, DC. title: Fully Funded Green RISE Africa Fellowship 2026: Empowering Climate Entrepreneurs Across Africa
summary: Looking to take your climate-focused enterprise to the next level? The Acumen Academy is now accepting applications for the Green RISE Africa Fellowship 2026. This fully funded leadership program supports entrepreneurs across East and West Africa who are building solutions at the intersection of climate action and poverty reduction. Designed for emerging leaders driving inclusive green growth, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to strengthen your leadership, scale your enterprise, and join a global community of changemakers. Applications open on 11 May 2026 , with the program starting in July 2026. About the Opportunity The Green RISE Africa Fellowship (Resilient Impact through Sustainable Entrepreneurship) is a six-month hybrid leadership program that equips entrepreneurs with the tools, frameworks, and networks needed to grow sustainable, climate-aligned businesses. Unlike traditional business programs, this fellowship focuses on moral leadership —helping participants develop the values, mindset, and decision-making skills required to create long-term, inclusive impact. Fellows remain in their current roles while engaging in virtual learning, in-person immersives, and peer collaboration. The program is designed to support entrepreneurs who are not only building businesses, but also creating dignified work opportunities , particularly for women and youth in underserved communities. East vs West Africa Fellowship The fellowship is delivered through two regional tracks, each tailored to specific contexts while maintaining a shared mission of building an inclusive green economy. West Africa Fellowship This track focuses on entrepreneurs working to expand green job opportunities for young rural women and youth across West Africa. It emphasizes building enterprises rooted in dignity, equity, and sustainable growth. Participants engage in immersive learning experiences, including in-person sessions in Nigeria , while connecting with a diverse network of peers tackling similar challenges across the region. East Africa Fellowship The East Africa track is open to entrepreneurs in countries such as Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Ethiopia, Burundi, South Sudan, and Somalia. It focuses on strengthening climate-aligned business models , improving resilience, and scaling impact across communities. Fellows participate in in-person immersives in Kenya and benefit from access to regional and global expertise. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa is at the forefront of both climate vulnerability and climate innovation. Entrepreneurs across the continent are developing solutions in renewable energy, agriculture, waste management, and more—but often face barriers in scaling their impact. The Green RISE Africa Fellowship addresses this gap by combining leadership development with practical enterprise support . It helps participants navigate complex challenges, engage stakeholders effectively, and build systems that prioritize both environmental sustainability and social inclusion. By fostering a strong community of leaders, the program also creates opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and long-term partnerships that extend far beyond the fellowship itself. What You’ll Gain Participants benefit from a comprehensive and fully funded experience, including: A structured 6-month leadership development program Access to Acumen’s global alumni network, The Foundry (2,000+ members) Training in key areas such as adaptive leadership, storytelling, and inclusive business strategies Participation in virtual sessions and in-person immersives Personalized mentorship and peer learning opportunities Continued engagement in a climate-focused community of practice after the program In addition, fellows may gain access to future funding and growth opportunities to further scale their enterprises. Program Structure The fellowship is designed to be flexible, requiring approximately 10 hours per month . It includes: A 3-day virtual orientation A 2-day virtual workshop Two 5-day in-person immersives Monthly cohort sessions and learning labs Self-paced assignments and one-on-one support This structure allows participants to immediately apply what they learn to their own enterprises. Eligibility This opportunity is ideal for individuals who: Are building or leading a green enterprise Focus on creating inclusive economic opportunities Are committed to addressing climate change and poverty Demonstrate leadership potential and a strong vision for impact Are based in eligible East or West African countries Application Process and Timeline To apply, candidates must: Review the Applicant Toolkit Submit an online application (opens 11 May 2026) Participate in a Selection Conference (4–6 June 2026) if shortlisted Begin the fellowship in July 2026 If you are ready to grow your enterprise and contribute to Africa’s green future, visit the official website to apply: See the West Africa Fellowship See the East Africa Fellowship Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one?
